เปิดใช้การค้นหา
ค้นหา < Close Search Panel ค้นหาขั้นสูง
ปิด 
[ ลงชื่อเข้าใช้](javascript:void\(\);) เพื่อเข้าถึงเนื้อหาที่มีการจำกัดการเข้าถึง 
### ใช้งานการค้นหาของ Intel.com
คุณสามารถค้นหาสิ่งต่าง ๆ ในเว็บไซต์ Intel.com ทั้งเว็บไซต์ได้หลายวิธี
  * ชื่อแบรนด์: **คอร์ i9**
  * หมายเลขเอกสาร: **123456**
  * Code Name: **Emerald Rapids**
  * ผู้ให้บริการพิเศษ: **“Ice Lake”, Ice AND Lake, Ice OR Lake, Ice***


### ลิงค์ด่วน
นอกจากนี้คุณยังสามารถลองลิงค์ด่วนด้านล่างเพื่อดูผลลัพธ์สำหรับการค้นหายอดนิยม
  * [ ข้อมูลผลิตภัณฑ์ ](https://www.thailand.intel.com/content/www/th/th/products/overview.html?wapkw=quicklink:products)
  * [ การสนับสนุน ](https://www.thailand.intel.com/content/www/th/th/support.html?wapkw=quicklink:suppor)
  * [ ไดรเวอร์และซอฟต์แวร์ ](https://downloadcenter.intel.com/th/?wapkw=quicklink:download-center)


### การค้นหาล่าสุด
[ ลงชื่อเข้าใช้](javascript:void\(\);) เพื่อเข้าถึงเนื้อหาที่มีการจำกัดการเข้าถึง 
### ค้นหาขั้นสูง
คำเหล่านี้ทั้งหมด คำใดคำหนึ่งเหล่านี้ ด้วยคำที่ต้องการเท่านั้น ค้นหาผลลัพธ์ด้วย
ผลลัพธ์ทั้งหมด  ข้อมูลผลิตภัณฑ์  การสนับสนุน  ไดรเวอร์และซอฟต์แวร์  เอกสารและแหล่งข้อมูล  พาร์ทเนอร์  ชุมชน  องค์กร  แสดงผลลัพธ์จาก
### ค้นหาเฉพาะใน
ชื่อเรื่อง คำอธิบาย ID คอนเทนต์
ค้นหา

  1. Research Center Partnerships


ไม่แนะนําให้ใช้เบราว์เซอร์รุ่นที่คุณใช้สําหรับไซต์นี้  
โปรดพิจารณาอัปเกรดเป็นเบราว์เซอร์เวอร์ชันล่าสุดโดยคลิกที่ลิงก์ต่อไปนี้

![Labs partnerhips](https://intelcorp.scene7.com/is/image/intelcorp/labs-partnerhips-marquee-berkeley-16x9:1920-1080?wid=1280&hei=720)
# Research Is Expanding the Future
Driving critical research in computer science through academic collaboration.
## Centers with Intel
Intel Labs sponsors various science and technology centers at universities around the world in order to foster collaborations and development of communities among Intel and academia. We also collaborate on initiatives with the National Science Foundation and the Semiconductor Research Corporation.  
  
| Research Center  | Description  |  
| --- | --- |  
| Visual Cloud Systems  | Centered at Carnegie Mellon University, Visual Cloud Systems aims to record and analyze the world's visual information so that computers (not humans) can understand and reason about it.  |  
| [Adversarial-Resilient Security Analytics (ARSA)](http://istc-arsa.iisp.gatech.edu/)  | Centered at Georgia Tech, Adversarial-Resilient Security Analytics will study the vulnerabilities of ML algorithms and develop new security approaches to improve the resilience of ML applications.  |  
| [Agile HW Design](https://aha.stanford.edu/)  | Colocated between UC Berkeley and Stanford University, the Agile Design center aims to enable are more agile hardware development flow, to quickly and easily modify and existing design.  |  
| Intelligent and Automated Connected Vehicles (IACV)  | Based in Beijing, China, this research center focuses on the safety of autonomous vehicles as well as the human-machine interface of autonomous vehicles and the challenges brought by the new supporting laws and regulations.  |  
| Internet of Everything  | Based in Taiwan at the National Taiwan University, this research center will serve as a conduit for research collaboration with the global and local industries to develop practical products and services.  |  
| Network on Intelligent Systems  | Based in Europe, this research center will address the major open problems in the design and deployment of intelligent systems that function in the physical world.  |  
| Collaborative and Autonomous Resilient Systems  | Based in Germany, this research center will investigate new opportunities for developing significant improvement to (a) the security of autonomous platforms as well as (b) self-defense capabilities of distributed systems.  |  
| Deep Learning IA  | This program with researchers from Berkeley, Stanford, and CMU is focused on advancing state of the art in Deep Learning while optimizing it for IA platforms.  |  
| FPGA Programming Optimization  | Making FPGAs more accessible to software developers and expanding the applicability of FPGAs across the compute continuum, from wearables to data centers and supercomputers.  |  
| Light Field Displays  | Developing mobile computing SoC architecture for eight-hour sustained operation and improve efficiency of power/performance by 10x for graphics, media and sensor IP.  |  
| Neuromorphic Architectures for Mainstream Computing  | Aims to extract key insights from neuroscience at the algorithmic level to provide guidance on future directions for neuromorphic computing architectures.  |  
| Programmable RF Filters  | Researching approaches to efficiently enable tunability of front end module (FEM) passive filters in mobile RF transceivers.  |  
| Ultra Low Power Radios for IoT  | Looks at approaches for enabling a new generation of ultra-low power (ULP) radios for active low-cost wireless sensor and compute platforms.  |  
[ ดูทั้งหมด ](https://www.thailand.intel.com/content/www/th/th/research/partnerships.html) [ แสดงน้อยลง ](https://www.thailand.intel.com/content/www/th/th/research/partnerships.html)
## Academic Affiliations  
| Research Center  | Description  |  
| --- | --- |  
| [Productive and Efficient Heterogeneous Programming (ASPIRE)](https://aspire.eecs.berkeley.edu/)  | Recognizes the shift from transistor-scaling-driven performance improvements to a new post-scaling world where whole-stack co-design is the key to improved efficiency.  |  
| [Berkeley Artificial Intelligence Research (BAIR) Lab](http://bair.berkeley.edu/)  | Brings together UC Berkeley researchers across the areas of computer vision, machine learning, natural language processing, planning, and robotics. BAIR includes over two dozen faculty and more than a hundred graduate students pursuing research on fundamental advances in the above areas as well as cross-cutting themes including multi-modal deep learning, human-compatible AI, and connecting AI with other scientific disciplines and the humanities.  |  
| [Data Analytics for What's Next (DAWN)](http://dawn.cs.stanford.edu/)  | Stanford based research project launched in 2017 to design systems and tools for usable machine learning, enabling non-experts to build and run production-ready ML apps.  |  
| [Real-time Intelligent Secure Execution (RISE)](https://rise.cs.berkeley.edu/)  | A 5-year research project focused on solving the systems, machine learning, and security challenges required to create an open-source, general-purpose, secure stack that can make intelligent decisions on live data in real-time.  |  
| [Stanford Data Sciences Initiative Affiliates Membership (SDSI)](https://sdsi.stanford.edu/)  | The Stanford Data Science Initiative (SDSI) is a university-wide organization focused on core data technologies with strong ties to application areas across campus. SDSI comprises methods research, infrastructure, and education.  |  
[ ดูทั้งหมด ](https://www.thailand.intel.com/content/www/th/th/research/partnerships.html) [ แสดงน้อยลง ](https://www.thailand.intel.com/content/www/th/th/research/partnerships.html)
## Partnerships with the National Science Foundation  
| Research Center  | Description  |  
| --- | --- |  
| [Intel NSF: Visual and Experiential Computing - Graduating 2018](https://www.nsf.gov/funding/pgm_summ.jsp?pims_id=505085)  | Foster novel, multidisciplinary approaches that will transform the way people interact with visual information through mobile and wearable  
devices, autonomous machines, and semantically aware spaces.  |  
| [Intel NSF: Cyber-Physical Systems, Security, and Privacy](https://www.nsf.gov/funding/pgm_summ.jsp?pims_id=505047)  | Transform the way people interact with engineered systems and address threats stemming from increasing reliance on computer and communication technologies.  |  
| [Intel NSF: Information-Centric Networking in Wireless Edge Networks](https://www.nsf.gov/funding/pgm_summ.jsp?pims_id=505310)  | Seeks unique data network architectures featuring an information plane using an Information-Centric Networking (ICN) approach and addressing discovery, movement, delivery, management, and protection of information within a network, along with the abstraction of an underlying communication plane creating opportunities for new efficiencies and optimizations across communications technologies that could also address latency and scale requirements.  |  
| [Intel NSF: Computer Assisted Programming for Heterogeneous Architectures](https://www.nsf.gov/funding/pgm_summ.jsp?pims_id=505319)  | Addresses the problem of effective software development for diverse hardware architectures through groundbreaking university research that will lead to a significant, measurable leap in software development productivity by partially or fully automating software development tasks that are currently performed by humans.  |  
[ ดูทั้งหมด ](https://www.thailand.intel.com/content/www/th/th/research/partnerships.html) [ แสดงน้อยลง ](https://www.thailand.intel.com/content/www/th/th/research/partnerships.html)
## Semiconductor Research Corporation  
| Research Center  | Description  |  
| --- | --- |  
|  [JUMP - Joint University Microelectronics Program](https://www.src.org/program/JUMP/)  
 | Supporting long-term research focused on high performance, energy efficient microelectronics for end-to-end sensing and actuation, signal and information processing, communication, computing, and storage solutions that are cost-effective and secure.  |  
| [ASCENT](https://www.src.org/program/jump/ascent/)  | ASCENT focuses on demonstration of foundational material synthesis routes and device technologies, novel heterogeneous integration (package and monolithic) schemes to support the next era of “functional hyper-scaling”.  |  
| [ADA](https://www.src.org/program/jump/ada/)  | The ADA Center will reignite system design innovation by drawing on opportunities in application driven architecture and system-driven technology advances, with support from agile system design frameworks that encompass programming languages to implementation technologies.  |  
| [CBRIC](https://www.src.org/program/jump/cbric/)  | The mission of CBRIC is to deliver key advances in cognitive computing, with the goal of enabling a new generation of autonomous intelligent systems.  |  
|  [ComSenTer](https://www.src.org/program/jump/comsenter/)[](https://www.src.org/program/jump/comsenter/)  | ComSenTer will develop the technologies for a future cellular infrastructure using hubs with massive spatial multiplexing, providing 1-100Gb/s to the end user, and, with 100-1000 simultaneous independently-modulated beams, aggregate hubs capacities in the 10's of Tb/s.  |  
| [CONIX](https://www.src.org/program/jump/conix/)  | CONIX aims to provide a new middle tier of distributed computing that tightly couples the cloud and edge by pushing increased levels of autonomy and intelligence into the network.  |  
| [CRISP](https://www.src.org/program/jump/crisp/)  | The CRISP grand challenge is to significantly lower the effort barrier for every day programmers to achieve highly portable, “bare-metal,” and understandable performance across a wide range of heterogeneous, IMS architectures.  |  
| [nCORE](https://www.src.org/compete/ncore/)  | The nanoelectronic COmputing REsearch (nCORE) program funds collaborative university research in the U.S. to develop key technologies to enable novel computing and storage paradigms with long-term impact on the semiconductor, electronics, computing, and defense industries. The nCORE program supports the National Strategic Computing Initiative (NSCI) through government-industry-academia collaborations. It will be driven by fundamental research on emerging materials and devices with the potential to achieve significantly improved efficiency, enhanced performance, and new functionalities, beyond the capability of conventional CMOS technologies. The new program is built upon the learning from the Nanoelectronics Research Initiative (NRI).  |  
[ ดูทั้งหมด ](https://www.thailand.intel.com/content/www/th/th/research/partnerships.html) [ แสดงน้อยลง ](https://www.thailand.intel.com/content/www/th/th/research/partnerships.html)
## Graduated Centers  
| Research Center  | Description  |  
| --- | --- |  
| Embedded Computing  | Centered at CMU. Smart Headlights and AndyVision--a planogram generating robot--were both featured at Research at Intel Day in 2012 and were just a few of the many success stories highlighted during the life of the center. 2011 - 2014.  |  
| Intel-NTU Connected Context Computing Center  | This center aimed to create demonstrable machine-to-machine (M2M) technologies that can showcase the potential of these technologies to transform our everyday activities and environment. Located in Taiwan - NTU. 2010 - 2016.  |  
| Visual Computing  | Centered at Stanford University. The center sought to bring modern trends in computing (the cloud, crowd sourcing, hand-held computing) to bear on hybrids of computer graphics, animation, image understanding, and large-scale gaming. 2011-2015.  |  
| [Cloud Computing](http://www.istc-cc.cmu.edu/index.shtml)  | Centered at Carnegie Mellon University, the Cloud Computing research community researched critical new underlying technologies for cloud computing of the future. 2011-2016.  |  
| [Pervasive Computing](http://istc-pc.washington.edu/)  | Centered at the University of Washington, the Pervasive Computing research center brought together research leaders in wireless communication and sensing, AI and ML, computer vision, HCI, and security. 2011-2016.  |  
| [Secure Computing](http://scrub.cs.berkeley.edu/)  | Centered at the University of California, Berkeley, the Center for Secure Computing focuses on scientific research to make computing technology safe and secure for users. 2011 - 2016.  |  
| [Big Data](http://istc-bigdata.org/)  | Centered at MIT, the Big Data research center is exploring data analytics to support data-intensive discovery including database management, analytics, and visualization support. 2012-2017.  |  
| Software Defined Networks (SDN)  | Seeks to make networks more amenable to innovation by extending the benefits of SDN to carrier networks. Research vectors include SDN for carriers, processing traffic in software, services architecture, and deployment scenarios. 2014-2017.  |  
| Low Latency Architectures  | Develop innovative techniques to reducing memory latency, create low latency storage systems, and accelerate progress in general-purpose microarchitectures, and accelerator architectures. 2014-2017.  |  
| Egocentric Video  | Develop techniques for effectively summarizing the video egocentric cameras collect and develop solutions for extracting the useful information embedded in the raw data (first-person video, images, audio, and location) egocentric cameras collect and presenting this information to the user on demand. 2014-2017.  |  
| Compressive Sensing  | Developing novel algorithms, architectures, accelerators, circuits and power management techniques that optimally exploit randomized compressive measurements and compressed domain data processing for 2D/3D still/video/MRI images. 2014-2017.  |  
| [IVCI Visual Computing](http://www.intel-vci.uni-saarland.de/en.html)  | Hubbed in Germany at Saarland University, the Institute focused on Visual Computing research, meaning the acquisition, modeling, processing, transmission, rendering, and display of visual and associated data. 2010-2016.  |  
| [Urban IoT](http://cities.io/)  | Based in England at University College London, Imperial College London, and Future Cities Catapult this center researches the compute fabric needed to support an urban Internet of Things at city scale. 2012-2017.  |  
| [Secure IoT Computing](http://www.icri-sc.org/institute/index.html)  | Based in Germany at TU Darmstadt, this center explores lightweight, cost-effective security and trust anchor primitives for IoT edge devices with integrated outputs into flexible and agile silicon prototypes. 2013-2017.  |  
| [Computational Intelligence](https://www.technion.ac.il/en/home-2/)  | Based in Israel at Technion and Hebrew University, this center focuses on hardware/software innovations for accelerating Machine Learning and Cognitive Applications. 2013-2017.  |  
| Mobile Networking and Computing  | Based in China at Tsinghua University, the research center for Mobile Networking and Computing is exploring advanced mobile network technologies to support typical applications in the next generation (5G) networks. 2015-2018.  |  
[ ดูทั้งหมด ](https://www.thailand.intel.com/content/www/th/th/research/partnerships.html) [ แสดงน้อยลง ](https://www.thailand.intel.com/content/www/th/th/research/partnerships.html)

เทคโนโลยี Intel อาจต้องใช้การเปิดใช้ฮาร์ดแวร์ ซอฟต์แวร์ หรือบริการ // ไม่มีผลิตภัณฑ์หรือส่วนประกอบใดที่จะปลอดภัยอย่างสมบูรณ์แบบ // ค่าใช้จ่ายและผลลัพธ์ของคุณอาจแตกต่างกันไป // ประสิทธิภาพแตกต่างกันไปขึ้นอยู่กับการใช้งาน การกำหนดค่า และปัจจัยอื่น ๆ เรียนรู้เพิ่มเติมได้ที่ _[intel.com/performanceindex](https://edc.intel.com/content/www/us/en/products/performance/benchmarks/overview/)_ // ดูประกาศและข้อสงวนสิทธิ์[ _ทางกฎหมายแบบสมบูรณ์ของเรา_](https://edc.intel.com/content/www/us/en/products/performance/benchmarks/overview/#GUID-26B0C71C-25E9-477D-9007-52FCA56EE18C) // Intel มุ่งมั่นที่จะให้ความเคารพในสิทธิมนุษยชน และหลีกเลี่ยงการมีส่วนร่วมในการละเมิดสิทธิมนุษยชน ดูหลักการด้านสิทธิมนุษยชนระดับโลก[ _ของ Intel_](https://www.intel.com/content/www/th/th/policy/policy-human-rights.html) ผลิตภัณฑ์และซอฟต์แวร์ Intel ผลิตมาเพื่อใช้เฉพาะในแอปพลิเคชันที่ไม่เป็นเหตุหรือมีส่วนให้เกิดการละเมิดต่อสิทธิมนุษยชนที่ยอมรับในระดับสากล
[ ![โลโก้ท้ายหน้าของ Intel](https://www.thailand.intel.com/content/dam/logos/intel-footer-logo.svg) ](https://www.thailand.intel.com/content/www/th/th/homepage.html "โลโก้ท้ายหน้าของ Intel")

ใช้งานการค้นหาของ Intel.com

คุณสามารถค้นหาสิ่งต่าง ๆ ในเว็บไซต์ Intel.com ทั้งเว็บไซต์ได้หลายวิธี

  • ชื่อแบรนด์: คอร์ i9
  • หมายเลขเอกสาร: 123456
  • Code Name: Emerald Rapids
  • ผู้ให้บริการพิเศษ: “Ice Lake”, Ice AND Lake, Ice OR Lake, Ice*

ลิงค์ด่วน

  1. Research Center Partnerships

ไม่แนะนําให้ใช้เบราว์เซอร์รุ่นที่คุณใช้สําหรับไซต์นี้
โปรดพิจารณาอัปเกรดเป็นเบราว์เซอร์เวอร์ชันล่าสุดโดยคลิกที่ลิงก์ต่อไปนี้

Research Is Expanding the Future

Driving critical research in computer science through academic collaboration.

Centers with Intel

Intel Labs sponsors various science and technology centers at universities around the world in order to foster collaborations and development of communities among Intel and academia. We also collaborate on initiatives with the National Science Foundation and the Semiconductor Research Corporation.

Research Center Description
Visual Cloud Systems Centered at Carnegie Mellon University, Visual Cloud Systems aims to record and analyze the world's visual information so that computers (not humans) can understand and reason about it.
Adversarial-Resilient Security Analytics (ARSA) Centered at Georgia Tech, Adversarial-Resilient Security Analytics will study the vulnerabilities of ML algorithms and develop new security approaches to improve the resilience of ML applications.
Agile HW Design Colocated between UC Berkeley and Stanford University, the Agile Design center aims to enable are more agile hardware development flow, to quickly and easily modify and existing design.
Intelligent and Automated Connected Vehicles (IACV) Based in Beijing, China, this research center focuses on the safety of autonomous vehicles as well as the human-machine interface of autonomous vehicles and the challenges brought by the new supporting laws and regulations.
Internet of Everything Based in Taiwan at the National Taiwan University, this research center will serve as a conduit for research collaboration with the global and local industries to develop practical products and services.
Network on Intelligent Systems Based in Europe, this research center will address the major open problems in the design and deployment of intelligent systems that function in the physical world.
Collaborative and Autonomous Resilient Systems Based in Germany, this research center will investigate new opportunities for developing significant improvement to (a) the security of autonomous platforms as well as (b) self-defense capabilities of distributed systems.
Deep Learning IA This program with researchers from Berkeley, Stanford, and CMU is focused on advancing state of the art in Deep Learning while optimizing it for IA platforms.
FPGA Programming Optimization Making FPGAs more accessible to software developers and expanding the applicability of FPGAs across the compute continuum, from wearables to data centers and supercomputers.
Light Field Displays Developing mobile computing SoC architecture for eight-hour sustained operation and improve efficiency of power/performance by 10x for graphics, media and sensor IP.
Neuromorphic Architectures for Mainstream Computing Aims to extract key insights from neuroscience at the algorithmic level to provide guidance on future directions for neuromorphic computing architectures.
Programmable RF Filters Researching approaches to efficiently enable tunability of front end module (FEM) passive filters in mobile RF transceivers.
Ultra Low Power Radios for IoT Looks at approaches for enabling a new generation of ultra-low power (ULP) radios for active low-cost wireless sensor and compute platforms.
ดูทั้งหมด แสดงน้อยลง

Academic Affiliations

Research Center Description
Productive and Efficient Heterogeneous Programming (ASPIRE) Recognizes the shift from transistor-scaling-driven performance improvements to a new post-scaling world where whole-stack co-design is the key to improved efficiency.
Berkeley Artificial Intelligence Research (BAIR) Lab Brings together UC Berkeley researchers across the areas of computer vision, machine learning, natural language processing, planning, and robotics. BAIR includes over two dozen faculty and more than a hundred graduate students pursuing research on fundamental advances in the above areas as well as cross-cutting themes including multi-modal deep learning, human-compatible AI, and connecting AI with other scientific disciplines and the humanities.
Data Analytics for What's Next (DAWN) Stanford based research project launched in 2017 to design systems and tools for usable machine learning, enabling non-experts to build and run production-ready ML apps.
Real-time Intelligent Secure Execution (RISE) A 5-year research project focused on solving the systems, machine learning, and security challenges required to create an open-source, general-purpose, secure stack that can make intelligent decisions on live data in real-time.
Stanford Data Sciences Initiative Affiliates Membership (SDSI) The Stanford Data Science Initiative (SDSI) is a university-wide organization focused on core data technologies with strong ties to application areas across campus. SDSI comprises methods research, infrastructure, and education.
ดูทั้งหมด แสดงน้อยลง

Partnerships with the National Science Foundation

Research Center Description
Intel NSF: Visual and Experiential Computing - Graduating 2018 Foster novel, multidisciplinary approaches that will transform the way people interact with visual information through mobile and wearable
devices, autonomous machines, and semantically aware spaces.
Intel NSF: Cyber-Physical Systems, Security, and Privacy Transform the way people interact with engineered systems and address threats stemming from increasing reliance on computer and communication technologies.
Intel NSF: Information-Centric Networking in Wireless Edge Networks Seeks unique data network architectures featuring an information plane using an Information-Centric Networking (ICN) approach and addressing discovery, movement, delivery, management, and protection of information within a network, along with the abstraction of an underlying communication plane creating opportunities for new efficiencies and optimizations across communications technologies that could also address latency and scale requirements.
Intel NSF: Computer Assisted Programming for Heterogeneous Architectures Addresses the problem of effective software development for diverse hardware architectures through groundbreaking university research that will lead to a significant, measurable leap in software development productivity by partially or fully automating software development tasks that are currently performed by humans.
ดูทั้งหมด แสดงน้อยลง

Semiconductor Research Corporation

Research Center Description
JUMP - Joint University Microelectronics Program
 Supporting long-term research focused on high performance, energy efficient microelectronics for end-to-end sensing and actuation, signal and information processing, communication, computing, and storage solutions that are cost-effective and secure.
ASCENT ASCENT focuses on demonstration of foundational material synthesis routes and device technologies, novel heterogeneous integration (package and monolithic) schemes to support the next era of “functional hyper-scaling”.
ADA The ADA Center will reignite system design innovation by drawing on opportunities in application driven architecture and system-driven technology advances, with support from agile system design frameworks that encompass programming languages to implementation technologies.
CBRIC The mission of CBRIC is to deliver key advances in cognitive computing, with the goal of enabling a new generation of autonomous intelligent systems.
ComSenTer ComSenTer will develop the technologies for a future cellular infrastructure using hubs with massive spatial multiplexing, providing 1-100Gb/s to the end user, and, with 100-1000 simultaneous independently-modulated beams, aggregate hubs capacities in the 10's of Tb/s.
CONIX CONIX aims to provide a new middle tier of distributed computing that tightly couples the cloud and edge by pushing increased levels of autonomy and intelligence into the network.
CRISP The CRISP grand challenge is to significantly lower the effort barrier for every day programmers to achieve highly portable, “bare-metal,” and understandable performance across a wide range of heterogeneous, IMS architectures.
nCORE The nanoelectronic COmputing REsearch (nCORE) program funds collaborative university research in the U.S. to develop key technologies to enable novel computing and storage paradigms with long-term impact on the semiconductor, electronics, computing, and defense industries. The nCORE program supports the National Strategic Computing Initiative (NSCI) through government-industry-academia collaborations. It will be driven by fundamental research on emerging materials and devices with the potential to achieve significantly improved efficiency, enhanced performance, and new functionalities, beyond the capability of conventional CMOS technologies. The new program is built upon the learning from the Nanoelectronics Research Initiative (NRI).
ดูทั้งหมด แสดงน้อยลง

Graduated Centers

Research Center Description
Embedded Computing Centered at CMU. Smart Headlights and AndyVision--a planogram generating robot--were both featured at Research at Intel Day in 2012 and were just a few of the many success stories highlighted during the life of the center. 2011 - 2014.
Intel-NTU Connected Context Computing Center This center aimed to create demonstrable machine-to-machine (M2M) technologies that can showcase the potential of these technologies to transform our everyday activities and environment. Located in Taiwan - NTU. 2010 - 2016.
Visual Computing Centered at Stanford University. The center sought to bring modern trends in computing (the cloud, crowd sourcing, hand-held computing) to bear on hybrids of computer graphics, animation, image understanding, and large-scale gaming. 2011-2015.
Cloud Computing Centered at Carnegie Mellon University, the Cloud Computing research community researched critical new underlying technologies for cloud computing of the future. 2011-2016.
Pervasive Computing Centered at the University of Washington, the Pervasive Computing research center brought together research leaders in wireless communication and sensing, AI and ML, computer vision, HCI, and security. 2011-2016.
Secure Computing Centered at the University of California, Berkeley, the Center for Secure Computing focuses on scientific research to make computing technology safe and secure for users. 2011 - 2016.
Big Data Centered at MIT, the Big Data research center is exploring data analytics to support data-intensive discovery including database management, analytics, and visualization support. 2012-2017.
Software Defined Networks (SDN) Seeks to make networks more amenable to innovation by extending the benefits of SDN to carrier networks. Research vectors include SDN for carriers, processing traffic in software, services architecture, and deployment scenarios. 2014-2017.
Low Latency Architectures Develop innovative techniques to reducing memory latency, create low latency storage systems, and accelerate progress in general-purpose microarchitectures, and accelerator architectures. 2014-2017.
Egocentric Video Develop techniques for effectively summarizing the video egocentric cameras collect and develop solutions for extracting the useful information embedded in the raw data (first-person video, images, audio, and location) egocentric cameras collect and presenting this information to the user on demand. 2014-2017.
Compressive Sensing Developing novel algorithms, architectures, accelerators, circuits and power management techniques that optimally exploit randomized compressive measurements and compressed domain data processing for 2D/3D still/video/MRI images. 2014-2017.
IVCI Visual Computing Hubbed in Germany at Saarland University, the Institute focused on Visual Computing research, meaning the acquisition, modeling, processing, transmission, rendering, and display of visual and associated data. 2010-2016.
Urban IoT Based in England at University College London, Imperial College London, and Future Cities Catapult this center researches the compute fabric needed to support an urban Internet of Things at city scale. 2012-2017.
Secure IoT Computing Based in Germany at TU Darmstadt, this center explores lightweight, cost-effective security and trust anchor primitives for IoT edge devices with integrated outputs into flexible and agile silicon prototypes. 2013-2017.
Computational Intelligence Based in Israel at Technion and Hebrew University, this center focuses on hardware/software innovations for accelerating Machine Learning and Cognitive Applications. 2013-2017.
Mobile Networking and Computing Based in China at Tsinghua University, the research center for Mobile Networking and Computing is exploring advanced mobile network technologies to support typical applications in the next generation (5G) networks. 2015-2018.

เทคโนโลยี Intel อาจต้องใช้การเปิดใช้ฮาร์ดแวร์ ซอฟต์แวร์ หรือบริการ // ไม่มีผลิตภัณฑ์หรือส่วนประกอบใดที่จะปลอดภัยอย่างสมบูรณ์แบบ // ค่าใช้จ่ายและผลลัพธ์ของคุณอาจแตกต่างกันไป // ประสิทธิภาพแตกต่างกันไปขึ้นอยู่กับการใช้งาน การกำหนดค่า และปัจจัยอื่น ๆ เรียนรู้เพิ่มเติมได้ที่  intel.com/performanceindex // ดูประกาศและข้อสงวนสิทธิ์ทางกฎหมายแบบสมบูรณ์ของเรา // Intel มุ่งมั่นที่จะให้ความเคารพในสิทธิมนุษยชน และหลีกเลี่ยงการมีส่วนร่วมในการละเมิดสิทธิมนุษยชน ดูหลักการด้านสิทธิมนุษยชนระดับโลกของ Intel ผลิตภัณฑ์และซอฟต์แวร์ Intel ผลิตมาเพื่อใช้เฉพาะในแอปพลิเคชันที่ไม่เป็นเหตุหรือมีส่วนให้เกิดการละเมิดต่อสิทธิมนุษยชนที่ยอมรับในระดับสากล
